title: They that Feared the Lord
summary: “Then they that feared the LORD spake often one to another: and the LORD hearkened, and heard it, and a book of remembrance was written before him for them that feared the LORD, and that thought upon his name.” (Malachi 3:16). Today, I feel like David with a burden for the young in particular, without neglecting the not so young. “Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the LORD” (Psalm 34:11). Notice firstly that David desired to teach the fear of the Lord. This means that the fear of the Lord does not come on its own but must be taught. Who can teach the fear of the Lord, then? The answer is those who have been taught the fear of the Lord by others and continue to live in the fear of the Lord; individuals such as David. David in turn learned the fear of God through his own mighty experiences in the fields of Bethlehem and under holy men of God such as Samuel and Nathan. Malachi 3:16 states that “they that feared the Lord spake often one to another”. Fear of the Lord is developed through fellowship where the Lord Jesus Christ is the center and the subject of discussion. Why does the church gather together? Simply because by talking often about the Lord we will increase in the fear of the Lord. If you are a regular churchgoer, lift up your hands and thank the good Lord for this blessed habit. It is not a small matter to be called a God-fearer.
